twitter_thread: 85% of AI agents are just API wrappers. I've seen 17 'agents' that just call the same 3 APIs.
Godcoder's local-first approach keeps your code on-machine, even during builds. 0 cloud calls.
Micro-Agents like Beat Frontier Models need 5x more collaboration to match a real AI Agent's output.
BYOL (Bring Your Own LLM key) saves $1,200/yr per user from vendor lock-in fees.
Eli-labz's Godcoder has 423 commits, 12 contributors. Real code, not just a press release.
7 out of 10 agents I've reviewed send your code to the cloud without consent. Check the EULA.
How many AI agents have you used that actually kept your code local? #aiagents #localfirst
pack.linkedin: 85% of AI agents are just API wrappers, with 17 examples calling the same 3 APIs. Godcoder's local-first approach is a step forward, keeping code on-machine. But what about the 7 out of 10 agents sending code to the cloud without consent? Using BYOL saves $1,200/yr per user from vendor lock-in. What's your experience with AI agents that keep code local?

pack.reddit_headline: 17 AI Agents, Same 3 APIs: Is the AI Agent Market Just a Bunch of API Wrappers?
